summary: CRITICAL REGRESSION verified on QR Preview Dialog (Vendors.js L652-L744). New iter-19 approach (body flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0 + sticky DialogFooter) does NOT produce the intended layout. On all 3 tested viewports (iPhone 15 Pro 393x852, iPhone SE 375x667, Desktop 1440x900) the sticky footer (Chiudi + Scarica PNG) is rendered IN THE MIDDLE of the visible content — physically OVERLAPPING the lower portion of the QR image AND positioned BEFORE the Link Landing Page info box (which is supposed to be BETWEEN the QR and the footer in DOM source order). Functional behavior still works (download triggers PNG, Chiudi closes modal, X close works, QR open button works) but the visual layout is broken. The screenshot user reported is reproduced 1:1 visually on the running app.

ui_bugs.issue: Sticky DialogFooter (bottom-0 from dialog.jsx default) overlaps the QR image and is rendered BEFORE the landing-info box visually, despite being LAST in DOM. On iPhone 15 Pro: img.bottom=477 > footer.top=408 (overlap by 69px). iPhone SE: img.bottom=392 > footer.top=248 (overlap by 144px). Desktop: img.bottom=528 > footer.top=510 (overlap by 18px).

The flex-1 body div (L666) lays out QR image and landing-info as expected internally, but because DialogContent itself has NO overflow-y on the OUTER box (only max-h + flex flex-col), the sticky child footer cannot anchor to a real scroll container relative to DialogContent. The sticky positioning resolves the footer at a non-deterministic mid-flow position, NOT at the bottom of DialogContent. Result: 'landing-info' (still inside body, AFTER QR) is rendered AFTER the footer visually.

issues: Visual order rendered: Header → QR image → Footer (Scarica/Chiudi) → Landing info box → empty space. Expected order: Header → QR → Landing info → Footer.
Lime green Scarica PNG button visually cuts through the QR image's bottom rows on iPhone15Pro and iPhoneSE (also visible on Desktop with smaller overlap).

action_items: REVERT iter-19 approach. The pattern 'body flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0 + sticky footer' only works when DialogContent itself is the scroll container (i.e., DialogContent needs overflow-hidden + flex-col, body needs overflow-y-auto + flex-1+min-h-0, footer needs to be a NORMAL flow child — NOT sticky). The current dialog.jsx DialogFooter default 'sticky bottom-0' fights with this pattern because the sticky resolves outside the body's scroll context.

Option A (LOCAL): In Vendors.js L728, override DialogFooter className to a non-sticky variant (e.g. className='!static !mx-0 !px-0 !border-t-0 !bg-transparent !pt-3') and KEEP the new body wrapper. The body is now the scroll surface; footer at natural flow position will be below the body which is shrunken by flex-1+min-h-0. This is essentially iter-18's working approach, kept now that the body is properly scrollable. (Note: original iter-18 issue was that body could exceed the dialog because no internal scroll — adding the body scroll wrapper here fixes that.)

Option B (GLOBAL): Refactor /app/frontend/src/components/ui/dialog.jsx — make DialogContent set 'overflow-hidden' on its OUTER box and stop relying on sticky in the footer default. Make footer position:static and rely on a flex-1+min-h-0+overflow-y-auto body wrapper. Then ALL dialogs using long bodies (PushBroadcastDialog, QrPreviewDialog, future ones) work consistently without per-dialog overrides.

After applying fix, re-run the same viewports and assert BOTH: (a) img.bottom < footer.top (no overlap with QR), (b) landing.bottom < footer.top (landing-info above footer), (c) footer.bottom <= viewport.height.
critical_code_review_comments: dialog.jsx L67 — DialogFooter default 'sticky bottom-0' is the root cause of repeated regressions (iter 8, iter 18, iter 19). The 'sticky bottom-0' only works correctly when the IMMEDIATE PARENT (DialogContent) is the scroll container. DialogContent currently has 'flex flex-col + max-h-[calc(100dvh-2rem)]' but NO overflow-hidden/auto on itself → DialogContent is NOT a scroll container → sticky resolves to viewport/document, which is unrelated to dialog box height → footer renders at unpredictable mid-flow position when body is shorter than max-h.

Vendors.js L666 — Adding body wrapper with 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0' is correct in principle BUT it does not turn DialogContent into a scroll container; it only makes the body itself one. The sticky footer (sibling of body, inside DialogContent) does NOT see the body's scroll context. This is why the new fix did not work.

Recommendation: convert DialogContent in dialog.jsx to 'overflow-hidden flex flex-col' + change DialogFooter default to non-sticky. Then every Dialog using a long body wraps it in 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0' (or a new shared DialogBody helper). This is the only durable architectural fix.

success_rate.frontend: 33% — functional checks PASS (download, close, X-close, open), layout assertion FAIL on all 3 viewports (no-overlap criterion violated)

test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (QA Test Org). Login OK.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Measurements (px, getBoundingClientRect on real preview URL):
- iPhone 15 Pro 393x852: dialog 83→769 (h=686). img 186→477. landing_url p 542→579. footer 408→509 (sticky, position:sticky bottom:0px). body 160→635 (h=475, scrollH=475, clientH=475, overflowY:auto — NOT scrolling because content fits). OVERLAP img↔footer = TRUE (477>408). OVERLAP landing↔footer = TRUE (579>408 → landing visually below footer but it's actually inside body which renders above footer, mid-flow inversion).
- iPhone SE 375x667: dialog 16→651. img 119→392. landing 510. footer 248.5→349.5 (sticky). body h=424 scrollH=473 → body IS scrollable. STILL overlap img↔footer (392>248).
- Desktop 1440x900: dialog 116→783. img 227→527. landing 629. footer 510→566. body h=484 scrollH=484. Overlap 528>510 (18px, visible in screenshot as lime button cutting through QR bottom).
Functional: Scarica PNG download triggers correctly (filename qr_<vendor_id>.png). Chiudi closes dialog. X (top-right) closes dialog. QR open button (m-qr-<id>) opens dialog. NO functional regression — purely a layout bug.
Screenshot of Desktop saved at /tmp/iphone15_qr_open.png (similar visual on all viewports — footer bar appears BETWEEN QR and landing info).
Key insight: the sticky footer + body-with-overflow approach needs DialogContent to be the scroll surface (overflow-hidden on DialogContent itself), not body. Current dialog.jsx DialogContent does NOT have overflow-hidden, so the architectural pattern fails.
rca of the issue:
Root cause: `position: sticky bottom-0` on DialogFooter (default in dialog.jsx L67) requires its NEAREST SCROLLING ANCESTOR to be the dialog box (DialogContent). DialogContent currently uses 'flex flex-col + max-h-[calc(100dvh-2rem)]' but has NO 'overflow-hidden' or 'overflow-y-auto' — therefore DialogContent is NOT a scroll container. When iter-19 wrapped the body in 'flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0', the BODY became a scroll container but the FOOTER (sibling, not child) does not see body's scroll context. The footer's sticky resolves to the next outer scroll ancestor (document/viewport), and because the dialog is position:fixed (transformed and centered), the resolution produces a footer position somewhere in the MIDDLE of DialogContent's flex flow, OVERLAPPING the body content.

Reproduction: 1) login admin@example.com/admin123, 2) /dashboard/vendors, 3) click any QR button (m-qr-<id> on mobile, d-qr-<id> on desktop), 4) inspect element on the white footer bar with Scarica PNG: position=sticky bottom=0px, but renders mid-dialog. The QR image's lower edge is HIDDEN behind the footer's white background. Below the footer the 'Link Landing Page' box (still child of body div) is rendered.

Mitigation: see action_items — either revert to iter-18's static-footer override (now safe because body wrapper provides internal scroll), OR globally refactor dialog.jsx so DialogContent has overflow-hidden + flex-col and DialogFooter default is non-sticky.
